From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

2012 single by Woodkid

"**Run Boy Run**" is a song by [Yoann Lemoine](/wiki/Yoann_Lemoine "Yoann Lemoine"), under his stage name Woodkid. It was released as the second single from his debut studio album, *[The Golden Age](/wiki/The_Golden_Age_(Woodkid_album) "The Golden Age (Woodkid album)")* (2013). It was written by Lemoine and Ambroise Willaume from the French band [Revolver](/wiki/Revolver_(French_band) "Revolver (French band)"). The single was released on 21 May 2012, becoming his highest-charting single. "Run Boy Run" was certified gold in Germany in 2014.[[1]](#cite_note-1) The song has been described as a [LGBTQ anthem](/wiki/LGBTQ_music "LGBTQ music").[[2]](#cite_note-Mouriquand-2024-2)

The music video for "Run Boy Run" was nominated for [Best Short Form Music Video](/wiki/Grammy_Award_for_Best_Short_Form_Music_Video "Grammy Award for Best Short Form Music Video") at the [2013 Grammy Awards](/wiki/2013_Grammy_Awards "2013 Grammy Awards").[[3]](#cite_note-grammys-3)

## Music video

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Run_Boy_Run_(song)&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Music video")]

The music video shows a young boy running, surrounded by various beasts that pick him up when he falls, hand him a sword and a shield, and put a horned helmet on his head. The video was directed by Lemoine himself.[[3]](#cite_note-grammys-3)

## In popular culture

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Run_Boy_Run_(song)&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: In popular culture")]

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  | Some of this section's [listed sources](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citing_sources "Wikipedia:Citing sources") **may not be [reliable](/wiki/Wikipedia:Reliable_sources "Wikipedia:Reliable sources")**. Please help improve this article by looking for better, more reliable sources. Unreliable citations may be challenged and removed. *(June 2023)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |

* "Run Boy Run" was used in several [O2](/wiki/O2_(United_Kingdom) "O2 (United Kingdom)") adverts (voiced by [Sean Bean](/wiki/Sean_Bean "Sean Bean"))[[4]](#cite_note-4)[[5]](#cite_note-5) and in a [Vodafone](/wiki/Vodafone "Vodafone") advert.[[6]](#cite_note-6)
* It was used in the trailer for the BBC One series *[The Musketeers](/wiki/The_Musketeers "The Musketeers")*.[[7]](#cite_note-7)
* The song has been used in the films *[The Maze Runner](/wiki/The_Maze_Runner_(film) "The Maze Runner (film)")* and *[Divergent](/wiki/Divergent_(film) "Divergent (film)")*.[[2]](#cite_note-Mouriquand-2024-2)
* It was used in the trailer for the final series of the BBC Three series *[Being Human](/wiki/Being_Human_(British_TV_series) "Being Human (British TV series)")*.[[8]](#cite_note-8)
* It was used in ads for the Power Horse energy drink in the Middle East and North Africa.[[9]](#cite_note-9)
* The song was used for a [Paso Doble](/wiki/Pasodoble "Pasodoble") dance by [Bethany Mota](/wiki/Bethany_Mota "Bethany Mota") and [Derek Hough](/wiki/Derek_Hough "Derek Hough") on the show *[Dancing with the Stars](/wiki/Dancing_with_the_Stars_(American_TV_series) "Dancing with the Stars (American TV series)")*.[[10]](#cite_note-10)
* It was used in certain [Cancer Research UK](/wiki/Cancer_Research_UK "Cancer Research UK") adverts throughout 2014–15.[[11]](#cite_note-11)
* Dance troupe Khronos Agoria used the song for their semi-final performance on [series 10](/wiki/Britain%27s_Got_Talent_series_10 "Britain's Got Talent series 10") of *[Britain's Got Talent](/wiki/Britain%27s_Got_Talent "Britain's Got Talent")* in May 2016[[12]](#cite_note-12) and Angara Contortion group used it for their audition on [series 11](/wiki/Britain%27s_Got_Talent_series_11 "Britain's Got Talent series 11") in May 2017.[[13]](#cite_note-13)
* It was used during the [Wind Games 2017](/wiki/Wind_Games "Wind Games") by [Kyra Poh](/wiki/Kyra_Poh "Kyra Poh") whose performance won the Gold in Solo Freestyle.[[14]](#cite_note-14)[[15]](#cite_note-15)[[16]](#cite_note-16)
* It was used in Episode 14 of the third season of *[How to Get Away with Murder](/wiki/How_to_Get_Away_with_Murder "How to Get Away with Murder")*.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]
* It was used in the ending of Episode 2 of the [Netflix](/wiki/Netflix "Netflix") show *[13 Reasons Why](/wiki/13_Reasons_Why "13 Reasons Why")*.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]
* It was used in the beginning of Episode 2 of the [Netflix](/wiki/Netflix "Netflix") show [*The Umbrella Academy*](/wiki/The_Umbrella_Academy_(TV_series) "The Umbrella Academy (TV series)").[[17]](#cite_note-17)
* It was used in the 2017 racing game *[Gran Turismo Sport](/wiki/Gran_Turismo_Sport "Gran Turismo Sport")*. The song's instrumental version was used in the game's [E3 2016](/wiki/Electronic_Entertainment_Expo_2016 "Electronic Entertainment Expo 2016") trailer.[[18]](#cite_note-18)
* It was used in the trailer for the film *[The Post](/wiki/The_Post_(film) "The Post (film)")* directed by [Steven Spielberg](/wiki/Steven_Spielberg "Steven Spielberg").[[19]](#cite_note-19)
* A portion was used by the [Mandarins Drum and Bugle Corps](/wiki/Mandarins_Drum_and_Bugle_Corps "Mandarins Drum and Bugle Corps") in their 2019 program subTerra.[[20]](#cite_note-20)
* An instrumental version of the song is used in the trailer for the 2019 film *[The Death & Life of John F. Donovan](/wiki/The_Death_%26_Life_of_John_F._Donovan "The Death & Life of John F. Donovan")*.[[21]](#cite_note-21)
* In 2023, Irish singer [Samantha Mumba](/wiki/Samantha_Mumba "Samantha Mumba") added a cover of "Run Boy Run" during her performance at that year's [Mighty Hoopla](/wiki/Mighty_Hoopla "Mighty Hoopla") festival.[[22]](#cite_note-22)

## Use in politics

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Run_Boy_Run_(song)&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Use in politics")]

"Run Boy Run" was used at a demonstration against [same-sex marriage](/wiki/Same-sex_marriage "Same-sex marriage") by French anti-[LGBTQ](/wiki/LGBTQ_people "LGBTQ people") organization [La Manif pour tous](/wiki/La_Manif_pour_tous "La Manif pour tous") in 2016, which was criticized by Lemoine, who himself is a [gay man](/wiki/Gay_men "Gay men"). The song was later also used in a campaign video for the [Donald Trump 2024 presidential campaign](/wiki/Donald_Trump_2024_presidential_campaign "Donald Trump 2024 presidential campaign"). In response Lemoine stated that the use of the song was never authorized and called upon his label [Universal Music France](/wiki/Universal_Music_France "Universal Music France") to react against it.[[2]](#cite_note-Mouriquand-2024-2)
